content.problemStatement:
Chef has decided that he will cut down on his calorie intake. He will eat atmost XXX calories in a day.

Today, he already ate YYY sweets, each having ZZZ calories. Find out how many more calories he can eat. If he has already exceeded his limit, output −1-1−1.


Input Format

The first and only line of input contains 333 integers - X,YX, YX,Y and ZZZ.


Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line



−1-1−1 if Chef has exceeded his calorie limit
The amount of calories Chef can still eat if he has not exceeded it

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Chef was allowed to eat 888 calories. He already ate 2⋅4=82 \cdot 4 = 82⋅4=8. Therefore, he has 10−8=210 - 8 = 210−8=2 calories more till he hits his limit.

Explanation:
Chef was allowed to eat 888 calories, but he already ate 2⋅6=122 \cdot 6 = 122⋅6=12. Therefore, he has exceeded his limit already, and we print −1-1−1.
